title: Ports, Immutable Domain Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle
description: Modules import global pools and fetch directly, promises float, AbortSignals are replaced, mutable objects cross layers, and shutdown cannot enumerate active handles. Learners cumulatively produce explicitly owned TypeScript service graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Represent admitted identifiers, instants, URLs, states, and results as readonly values created only after runtime checks.
Assign every Promise a caller, awaited settlement, cancellation policy, error path, concurrency budget, and shutdown consequence.
